evidence_quotes.quote: games like Hitman 3 (World of assasination) require an internet connection to play it to its full potentional.
If they don't want to sell it, then they should have to EXPLICITLY make it clear that you are buying a license until they shut down, or that you are renting it.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I would appreciate it if you could maybe also include aspect of the "Stop Killing Games" movements. I believe that for a fair digital world, that when users buy a game, that they should be capable of playing it for the rest of time. This is not just about games, but games like Hitman 3 (World of assasination) require an internet connection to play it to its full potentional. If you are not logged in, your progress (including unlocked weapons/starting locations) will not be available. There is this company that has exercise bikes that only work properly if you have an online connection??? Just see this video where they talk about that. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4c5eX-tKyhc Both business practices result in destroying the thing you bought, if the company you bought the thing from goes out of business. And it is a horrendous idea. When I buy something, I want to own it. If they don't want to sell it, then they should have to EXPLICITLY make it clear that you are buying a license until they shut down, or that you are renting it. With kind regards, A concerned Dutch citizen
